name: usgs-earthquake
description: Get real-time earthquake data from the US Geological Survey. Returns recent seismic events worldwide with magnitude, location, depth, and tsunami alerts. Use for questions like 'any earthquakes today', 'recent earthquakes near Japan', 'biggest earthquakes this week'. Filter by magnitude, date range, and geographic area.

input.description: Query parameters as JSON. All optional. 'format' should be 'geojson'. 'starttime' and 'endtime' (ISO 8601 dates), 'minmagnitude' and 'maxmagnitude' (numbers), 'limit' (max results, default 20), 'orderby' ('time', 'magnitude'), 'latitude'+'longitude'+'maxradiuskm' (for geographic search).

maxradiuskm.description: Search radius in km (use with latitude/longitude)

output.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection with metadata (count, title) and features array. Each feature has properties (mag, place, time, tsunami, title, url) and geometry (coordinates as [lon, lat, depth]).
